near_leaf_pairs.note: Same call: the green 'Equities' main dial. Snapshot L45 'Equities' / L46 'We maintain our positive view on equities, fading near-term geopolitical risks... fundamentals remain supportive.' GT mapped it to the geo leaf Global Equities; model to the broad Equities - General leaf. Pure leaf-name drift, agreeing view.

Same dial: 'Emerging markets USD' under Investment grade credit. Snapshot L120-123 'Emerging markets USD... regional exposure to the Middle East and Asia means the risk of spreads widening is greater than current levels reflect.' Both commentaries quote the same downgrade. Model's leaf (EM IG Credit) tracks the dial's IG-credit section; GT mapped to sovereign hard-currency debt. Agreeing view.

model_only_judgments.reason: Prose view verified L25-28: 'We maintain our overweight position in equities but have decided to close the overweight to international value stocks as financials remain vulnerable to rate concerns and the US is likely to be more resilient.' Closing an overweight => neutral, so N is the correct read (fixes pilot-05's overreach at U). Stated view, not inferred; GT simply carries no value/style row.
Green 'Equities' main dial (L45-48). Same call GT records under the 'Global Equities' leaf; differs only by leaf convention (broad asset-allocation leaf vs geo leaf).

Red 'Government bonds' main dial verified L49-53 'Government bonds... We remain negative on government bonds.' Model emits the main-level dial; GT keeps only the regional sub-dials (US Treasuries U, UK Gilts N, etc.). Level convention difference.

Red/down 'Corporate bonds (credit)' main dial verified L58-60 'Corporate bonds (credit)... downgraded our score for credit to negative.' Model emits the broad credit dial; GT keeps only IG/HY sub-dials. Level convention difference.
'Emerging markets USD' dial verified L120-123. Same call GT records under 'EM Debt - Hard Currency'; differs only by leaf convention (IG-credit section vs sovereign hard-currency).

Yellow/down 'Energy' dial verified L132-136 'Energy... we switch to neutral while we wait for clarity on the duration/outcome of the war.' Correct capture of a real commodities sub-dial; GT kept siblings Gold (O) and Agriculture (N) but omitted Energy.

Yellow 'Industrial metals' dial verified L141-145 'Industrial metals... we do not see evidence of enough demand... to justify upgrading our view.' Copper/Industrial is the taxonomy's industrial-metals leaf; N is correct. GT omitted it while keeping other commodity sub-dials.

gt_only_root_causes.note: Model row 'Equities - General' O carries the identical claim from the green 'Equities' main dial (L45-48 'We maintain our positive view on equities... fundamentals remain supportive'). Same view; leaf-name drift only.

Model row 'EM IG Credit' U carries the same downgrade from the 'Emerging markets USD' dial (L120-123). Same view; the dial physically sits in the Investment grade credit section, so the model's leaf is arguably the more faithful mapping.

notes: Recall: strong. All 26 exact rows agree on view, and both gt_only rows resolve as near_leaf_covered with agreeing views (Global Equities<-Equities-General; EM Debt Hard Currency<-EM IG Credit) — so there are ZERO true misses (no not_emitted, no not_grounded, no inference_depth) and zero view disagreements. Overreach: NONE this run — pilot-05's two overreaches are gone. Notably 'Global ex-US Value' flipped from U (pilot-05 overreach) to N, which is the correct read of 'close the overweight to international value stocks' (closing an overweight => neutral). Convention: GT's level policy remains internally inconsistent — it drops the Government bonds and Corporate bonds MAIN dials (kept by model) in favor of sub-dials, yet keeps main dials for Equities/Commodities elsewhere; and it keeps Gold/Agriculture sub-dials while omitting the Energy and Industrial metals siblings the model captured. All such model_only rows are correct captures of visible dials or explicit prose. Inferred/thin audit: CONFIRMED — no basis:inferred and no checker_strength:thin rows; every Schroders model row is grounded in a visible views-grid dial or an explicit stated prose view (e.g. L25-28, L37), i.e. all stated and decisive-or-adequate. Review flags: all 7 model_only rows carry review_flag 'none', consistent with no overreach. The review doc emitted 0 candidates, defensible as narrative market commentary without a views grid.
